title: Microsoft vcpkg OpenSSL Uncontrolled Search Path Element Local Privilege Escalation Vulnerability
description:
This vulnerability allows local attackers to escalate privileges on applications built using the Microsoft vcpkg port of OpenSSL. An attacker must first obtain the ability to execute low-privileged code on the target system in order to exploit this vulnerability.
The specific flaw exists within the configuration of OpenSSL. The product loads configuration from an unsecured location. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to escalate privileges and execute arbitrary code in the context of a target process.
addtionnal_details: Microsoft has issued an update to correct this vulnerability. More details can be found at:https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/security/advisories/GHSA-p322-v6vw-vrq9
timeline:
2026-03-10 - Vulnerability reported to vendor
2026-04-15 - Coordinated public release of advisory
2026-04-15 - Advisory Updated
